description: Orders table foreign keys — review a migration adding constraints and indexes on high-throughput order_items table (25M rows, 1500 writes/sec) under rolling deployment
review_checklist: Check for NOT NULL columns added without DEFAULT values on large tables
Verify indexes are created with CONCURRENTLY on large tables
Check if dropped or renamed columns are still referenced by application code
Check for lock-heavy operations on high-traffic tables
Consider the deployment strategy (rolling/blue-green) and downtime budget

ground_truth.description: Adding NOT NULL column shipping_address_id without a DEFAULT on the orders table causes a full table rewrite with ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock, blocking all reads and writes including checkout_service's order creation flow.

Adding FK constraint on order_items (25M rows, 1500 writes/sec) validates the entire table with a SHARE ROW EXCLUSIVE lock. The supporting index is created after the constraint, forcing a sequential scan during validation.
Creating index without CONCURRENTLY on order_items (25M rows, 1500 writes/sec) acquires a SHARE lock, blocking all writes and causing massive write queue buildup in checkout_service.

Creating index without CONCURRENTLY on order_items (25M rows) acquires a SHARE lock blocking all writes. Combined with the other non-concurrent index on the same table, this compounds the total write-blocking duration.
